qa_pairs.question: What is the error message encountered when pip tries to uninstall a package in a read-only location?
qa_pairs.answer: The error message is "ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: [Errno 30] Read-only file system".

qa_pairs.question: When might a user encounter the "OSError: [Errno 30] Read-only file system" error?
qa_pairs.answer: This error may occur when installing packages in your notebook, specifically when pip tries to uninstall an existing package in a read-only location.

qa_pairs.question: How can I resolve the "OSError: [Errno 30] Read-only file system" when installing packages in a notebook cell?
qa_pairs.answer: You can use the command `pip install --no-index --ignore-installed <package>` in your notebook cell to install the package that caused the error.

qa_pairs.question: Does the solution `pip install --no-index --ignore-installed <package>` work for all packages?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this solution may not work for all packages.

qa_pairs.question: For which specific packages might the `pip install --no-index --ignore-installed` command not work?
qa_pairs.answer: The command may not work for packages such as `pyarrow`, `opencv`, and `mpi4py`.
